volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 1206 - Section 1289)

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.original: χρῶτʼ ἀπὸ πάντα καὶ ἄρθρα τέμω χερί ·
sections.translation: That you may execute what scheme ?

sections.label: Section 1207
sections.original: φονᾷ φονᾷ νόος ἤδη .
sections.translation: Mangle all this body , and sever limb from limb with my own hand ! Death , death is my thought now !

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: πατέρα ματεύων .
sections.translation: Why , why ever would you —

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: ποῖ γᾶς ;
sections.translation: I am seeking my father —

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ἐστʼ ἐν φάει γʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: In what land ?

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: λιβάδʼ ἐχθροῖς ἔβαν Δαναοῖς
sections.translation: for leaving your sacred stream and going to help the Danaans , my enemies ! I am nothing now , nothing anymore ! Exit Philoctetes into the cave .

sections.label: Section 1218
sections.original: στείχων ἂν ἦ σοι τῆς ἐμῆς , εἰ μὴ πέλας
sections.translation: Long ago I would have left you to go to my ship , had I not seen

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: γόνον πρὸς ἡμᾶς δεῦρʼ ἰόντʼ ἐλεύσσομεν .
sections.translation: Odysseus approaching , and the son of Achilles , too , coming here for us .

sections.label: Section 1222
sections.original: κέλευθον ἕρπεις ὧδε σὺν σπουδῇ ταχύς ;
sections.translation: Will you not tell me why you make this return journey with such eager speed ?

sections.label: Section 1224
sections.original: δεινόν γε φωνεῖς · ἡ δʼ ἁμαρτία τίς ἦν ;
sections.translation: I come to undo the mistake that I made earlier .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: ἣν σοὶ πιθόμενος τῷ τε σύμπαντι στρατῷ
sections.translation: Your words alarm me — what mistake was that ?

sections.label: Section 1226
sections.original: ἔπραξας ἔργον ποῖον ὧν οὔ σοι πρέπον ;
sections.translation: The one I made when I obeyed you and all the army .

sections.label: Section 1227
sections.original: ἀπάταισιν αἰσχραῖς ἄνδρα καὶ δόλοις ἑλών .
sections.translation: What did you do that was unworthy of you ?

sections.label: Section 1228
sections.original: τὸν ποῖον ; ὤμοι · μῶν τι βουλεύει νέον ;
sections.translation: I captured a man by disgraceful deceits and treachery .

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: νέον μὲν οὐδέν , τῷ δὲ Ποίαντος τόκῳ ,
sections.translation: Rash , no . But to Poeas ’ son —

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσεις ; ὥς μʼ ὑπῆλθέ τις φόβος .
sections.translation: What are you going to do ? Suddenly a certain fear comes over me .

sections.label: Section 1232
sections.original: παρʼ οὗπερ ἔλαβον τάδε τὰ τόξʼ , αὖθις πάλιν
sections.translation: — From whom I took this bow , back to him —

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τί λέξεις ; οὔ τί που δοῦναι νοεῖς ;
sections.translation: Zeus ! What will you say ? Certainly you do not intend to give it back ?

sections.label: Section 1234
sections.original: αἰσχρῶς γὰρ αὐτὰ κοὐ δίκῃ λαβὼν ἔχω .
sections.translation: Yes , I do , because disgracefully and unjustly I got hold of it .

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , πότερα δὴ κερτομῶν λέγεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: In the name of the gods , are you saying this to mock me ?

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: εἰ κερτόμησίς ἐστι τἀληθῆ λέγειν .
sections.translation: If it is mockery to speak the truth .

sections.label: Section 1237
sections.original: τί φής , Ἀχιλλέως παῖ ; τίνʼ εἴρηκας λόγον ;
sections.translation: What do you mean , Neoptolemus ? What are you saying ?

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: δὶς ταὐτὰ βούλει καὶ τρὶς ἀναπολεῖν μʼ ἔπη ;
sections.translation: Must I repeat the same words twice and three times ?

sections.label: Section 1239
sections.original: ἀρχὴν κλύειν ἂν οὐδʼ ἅπαξ ἐβουλόμην .
sections.translation: I would not have wished to hear them even once .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: εὖ νῦν ἐπίστω πάντʼ ἀκηκοὼς λόγον .
sections.translation: Know for certain that I have nothing more to say .

sections.label: Section 1241
sections.original: ἔστιν τις , ἔστιν ὅς σε κωλύσει τὸ δρᾶν .
sections.translation: There is someone , I tell you , who will prevent your deed .

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: τί φής ; τίς ἔσται μʼ οὑπικωλύσων τάδε ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Who will oppose me in this ?

sections.label: Section 1243
sections.original: ξύμπας Ἀχαιῶν λαός , ἐν δὲ τοῖς ἐγώ .
sections.translation: The whole host of the Achaeans , and I for one .

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: σοφὸς πεφυκὼς οὐδὲν ἐξαυδᾷς σοφόν .
sections.translation: Wise though you were born , your threats are void of wisdom .

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὔτε φωνεῖς οὔτε δρασείεις σοφά .
sections.translation: And your words are not wise , nor is that which you want to do .

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ δίκαια , τῶν σοφῶν κρείσσω τάδε .
sections.translation: And yet if they are just , they are better than wise .

sections.label: Section 1247
sections.original: καὶ πῶς δίκαιον , ἅ γʼ ἔλαβες βουλαῖς ἐμαῖς , πάλιν μεθεῖναι ταῦτα ;
sections.translation: And how is it just for you to give up what was won by means of my plans ?

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: στρατὸν δʼ Ἀχαιῶν οὐ φοβεῖ , πράσσων τάδε ;
sections.translation: The army of the Achaeans causes you no fear , when you do this ?

sections.label: Section 1251
sections.original: ξὺν τῷ δικαίῳ τὸν σὸν οὐ ταρβῶ φόβον .
sections.translation: With justice on my side , I do not tremble at the terrors you name .

sections.label: Section 1251a
sections.original: ξὺν τῷ δικαίῳ χεὶρ ἐμή σʼ ἀναγκάσει .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδέ τοι σῇ χειρὶ πείθομαι τὸ δρᾶν .
sections.translation: No , not even at the threat of your hand do I yield obedience .

sections.label: Section 1253
sections.original: οὔ τἄρα Τρωσίν , ἀλλὰ σοὶ μαχούμεθα .
sections.translation: Then our battles shall be not with the Trojans , but with you .

sections.label: Section 1254
sections.original: ἴτω τὸ μέλλον .
sections.translation: So be it , if that is what the future holds .

sections.label: Section 1254a
sections.original: χεῖρα δεξιὰν ὁρᾷς
sections.translation: Do you see my right hand

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: κώπης ἐπιψαύουσαν ;
sections.translation: clasping my sword hilt ?

sections.label: Section 1255a
sections.original: ἀλλὰ κἀμέ τοι
sections.translation: You will see me do the same , and not slowly .

sections.label: Section 1257
sections.original: καίτοι σʼ ἐάσω · τῷ δὲ σύμπαντι στρατῷ
sections.translation: However , I will let you be . But I will go report this to all the army , and by them you will be punished .

sections.label: Section 1259
sections.original: ἐσωφρόνησας · κἂν τὰ λοίφʼ οὕτω φρονῇς ,
sections.translation: You have come to your senses . And if you are so prudent hereafter ,

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: ἴσως ἂν ἐκτὸς κλαυμάτων ἔχοις πόδα .
sections.translation: perhaps you may steer clear of trouble . Odysseus feigns departure , but conceals himself nearby .

sections.label: Section 1261
sections.original: σὺ δʼ , ὦ Ποίαντος παῖ , Φιλοκτήτην λέγω ,
sections.translation: But you , son of Poeas , Philoctetes , come out ! Leave the shelter of your rocky home !

sections.label: Section 1263
sections.original: τίς αὖ παρʼ ἄντροις θόρυβος ἵσταται βοῆς ;
sections.translation: What is this disruptive cry once more rising beside my cave ? Why do you call me ? What do you want of me ? He appears at the mouth of the cave , and sees Neoptolemus .

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: ὤμοι · κακὸν τὸ χρῆμα . μῶν τί μοι νέα
sections.translation: Oh , no ! This business will bring me no good . Have you come bringing me new misery on top of the old ?

sections.label: Section 1267
sections.original: θάρσει · λόγους δʼ ἄκουσον οὓς ἥκω φέρων .
sections.translation: Take heart and listen to my words .

sections.label: Section 1268
sections.original: δέδοικʼ ἔγωγε · καὶ τὰ πρὶν γὰρ ἐκ λόγων
sections.translation: I am afraid . Beautiful words did me evil once before , when I believed your promises .

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: οὔκουν ἔνεστι καὶ μεταγνῶναι πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Is there no room , then , for repentance ?

sections.label: Section 1271
sections.original: τοιοῦτος ἦσθα τοῖς λόγοισι χὤτε μου
sections.translation: You spoke just like this , when you were seeking to steal my bow — a professed friend , with my destruction in his treacherous heart .

sections.label: Section 1273
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ τι μὴν νῦν · βούλομαι δέ σου κλύειν ,
sections.translation: I assure you , I am not so now . I merely wish to know whether you have resolved to stay here and endure , or to sail with us .

sections.label: Section 1275a
sections.original: παῦε , μὴ λέξῃς πέρα ·
sections.translation: Stop , not another word ! Whatever you may say will be said in vain .

sections.label: Section 1277
sections.original: οὕτω δέδοκται ;
sections.translation: You are so resolved ?

sections.label: Section 1277a
sections.original: καὶ πέρα γʼ ἴσθʼ ἢ λέγω .
sections.translation: More firmly , believe me , than words can say .

sections.label: Section 1278
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἤθελον μὲν ἄν σε πεισθῆναι λόγοις
sections.translation: Well , I could have wished that you had listened to my words , but if nothing that I say will help , then I am finished .

sections.label: Section 1280a
sections.original: πάντα γὰρ φράσεις μάτην .
sections.translation:
Yes , all your pleas will be in vain .
You will never gain my mind ’ s good will , since first you fraudulently seized my means of life and robbed me of it , and then you have come here to admonish me , you most hateful descendant of so noble a father !

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: ὄλοισθʼ , Ἀτρεῖδαι μὲν μάλιστʼ , ἔπειτα δὲ ὁ Λαρτίου παῖς καὶ σύ .
sections.translation: Ruin seize you all , the Atreids first , and next the son of Laertes , and you !

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ἆρα δεύτερον δολούμεθα ;
sections.translation: What did you say ? Am I being tricked a second time ?

sections.label: Section 1289
sections.original: ἀπώμοσʼ ἁγνὸν Ζηνὸς ὑψίστου σέβας .
sections.translation: No , I swear it by the pure majesty of Zeus most high !
